Default Prove-Up Hearing On The Cross-Complaint Filed By American Contractors Indemnity Company As To Cross-Defendant Charles A. Weidinger.
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to the cross-complaint of American Contractors Indemnity Company.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Charles Philipps, Esq. appeared for cross-complainant American Contractors Indemnity Company.
No appearance by cross-defendant Charles A. Weidinger.
Default was entered as to cross-defendant on July 31, 2009.
Roe cross-defendants were dismissed on October 9, 2009.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on December 15, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Cross-Complainant American Contractors Indemnity Company, and the Cross-Defaulted Defendant Charles A. Weidinger as follows:

Damages/Principal - $5,789.52.
Attorney's Fees - $3,397.00.
Costs - $419.35, for a total judgment in the amount of $,605.87.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
